{"id":"smallhold-22p","title":"FederationClient: reqwest + draft-cavage HTTP signature for GETs","description":"Implement the outbound HTTP client used for fetching remote actor documents and making signed requests. Uses reqwest with rustls-tls (NOT openssl). GET requests must be signed per draft-cavage-http-signatures-11: sign (request-target), host, date. Date header: RFC 7231 UTC format, must be within 30s of 'now'. Accept header: application/activity+json, application/ld+json; profile=...

Agent workflow: Spawn a research subagent to study how activitypub-federation crate handles signing, and whether we should use its HTTP client or wrap our own. Spawn a testing subagent to write a mock server that validates the signature format.

Test discipline:
- Stand up a local mock server (axum or wiremock) that validates incoming HTTP signatures
- Sign a request, send to mock server, verify: (1) Signature header present, (2) keyId ends with #main-key, (3) algorithm is rsa-sha256, (4) Date header within 30s, (5) headers parameter includes (request-target) host date
- Test clock skew: signature with Date 31s old — verify a real server (GoToSocial) rejects it

Defensive programming:
- Set a connection timeout (20s from config) and request timeout separately
- User-Agent header must always be set (from config) — missing User-Agent causes some servers to reject
- Never follow redirects silently — log and handle
- Never use HTTP (non-TLS) for production federation requests — fail with clear error","acceptance_criteria":"Signed GETs accepted by GoToSocial (requires authorized fetch); Date within 30s; keyId has #main-key fragment; reqwest uses rustls not openssl","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:29:18Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:29:18Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-23j","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:29:21Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
